examples.title: Locally Weighted Regression Pseudocode
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LocallyWeightedRegression
INPUT: training data X, y, query point x_q, bandwidth τ
OUTPUT: predicted value y_q

BEGIN
 FOR EACH query point x_q:
 COMPUTE weights w^(i) for each training example using Gaussian kernel
 MINIMIZE weighted cost function J(θ) = Σ w^(i) * (y^(i) - θ^T x^(i))^2
 FIT local model using weighted least squares
 MAKE prediction y_q = θ^T x_q
 DISCARD model
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the locally weighted regression algorithm. It iterates over each query point, computes weights for each training example using a Gaussian kernel, minimizes the weighted cost function, fits a local model using weighted least squares, makes a prediction, and discards the model.

examples.title: Locally Weighted Regression Python Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of Locally Weighted Regression
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the locally weighted regression algorithm. It defines a Gaussian kernel function and a locally weighted regression function, which computes weights, fits a local model using weighted least squares, and makes a prediction.
